# Verify GitHub Star or Rank Abuse

## Claim being tested

Stars/watchers/forks reflect genuine developer interest.

## Alternative hypothesis

Stars or follows were artificially inflated to manufacture developer legitimacy.

## Data fields

- stargazer_id
- star timestamp
- account creation date
- prior repo activity
- follower/following
- fork/issue/PR activity
- related repo stars
- external promotion window

## Metrics

- star burst z-score
- account-age distribution
- stargazer activity depth
- star-to-fork ratio
- star-to-issue/PR ratio
- common-star overlap across related repos
- suspicious account cluster density

## Safe conclusion

"Star growth shows rank-abuse risk if high star velocity is driven by thin accounts with little developer behaviour."
